pattern: To buy the most bars on a fixed budget you always take the cheapest remaining one, which after sorting the costs becomes a single prefix scan.
hints: Buying more cheap bars never costs you a chance at an expensive one, so prefer the cheapest first.
Sort the prices (or bucket them by value) and spend greedily from the low end.
After sorting, keep subtracting prices from coins until the next price exceeds the remaining coins; the count taken is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: budget exactly buys all but one

stress.description: many random prices and a random budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    costs = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    coins = rng.randint(1, 100000000)
    return [costs, coins]
